page: Amazon Web Services c8i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core allocation. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across multiple domains. The processor features advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for integer and tile operations, which support its strong Passmark CPU extended instructions score of 67602.78. Single-core performance is notable, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2196.0 placing it in the top tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching a strong read-only small block cached speed of 3842.33 GB/sec and a large block uncached speed of 195.86 GB/sec. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 is also strong at 338.07 GB/sec.

Given its compute-heavy profile and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the c8i.12xlarge is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring dense CPU resources without massive memory footprints. It is well-suited for software compilation, image processing, and text processing, where it achieves top-tier benchmark ratings. Additionally, its strong performance in Redis SET operations (88.09 million operations per second) and static web serving (81.79 GB/sec) makes it an excellent fit for high-throughput web serving, database operations, and intermediate LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors on the x86_64 architecture. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, offering 2.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPUs. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2196.0, and strong memory read bandwidth of up to 3842.33 GB/sec. Supported by Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, this instance is highly efficient for compute-intensive tasks. It is optimized for software compilation, image processing, high-throughput web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, delivering top-tier single-core performance and strong memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.12xlarge is an Intel-based compute-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density x86_64 compute instance featuring advanced matrix extensions, fast DDR5 memory, and optimized single-core processing performance.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores of Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
Equipped with 96.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Features Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instruction sets
Delivers 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Best suited for software compilation, image processing, and high-throughput web serving
